questions.question_text:
In 1966, a military Coup d'état overthrew the government of Dr. Kwame Nkrumah.

What was the main reason for the Coup?

options.B: External interference from the Russian government

questions.question_text: The destruction of farm lands by cattle and the confrontation between herdsmen and farming communities in Ghana can best be resolved by

options.A: local farmers always standing by to drive away herdsmen.
options.B: encouraging the practice of cattle ranching in our communities.
options.C: allowing the game and wildlife department to take charge of cattle rearing in the communities.
options.D: seizing and distributing some of the cattle to the affected communities.

questions.explanation: Ranching is the practice of raising herds of animals on large tracts of land.

questions.question_text: Ghana's public sector faces various challenges with respect to human resource development. Which of the following challenges is the most suitable challenge in the above given scenario?
Which type of map will be most suitable for determining the elevation of a mountain range?
questions.explanation: Topographic maps are detailed, accurate graphic representations of features that appear on the Earth's surface. These features include: cultural: roads, buildings, urban development, railways, airports, names of places and geographic features, administrative boundaries, state and international borde

questions.question_text: Which of the following military rulers of Ghana implemented the Economic Recovery Program (ERP)?
Which of the following scenarios explain the main purpose of the Internet?

options.A: Robots building several vehicles at a time
options.C: Connecting electronic devices and making lives more convenient
options.D: Scientists going to space and other planets

questions.question_text: There is an ongoing illegal mining activity in a community. The miners are using earth moving machines, cyanide and mercury. Sixty acres of vegetation cover is pulled down every hour. Which of the following options best describes the long term effect of their activities on the land?
questions.explanation: Vegetation cover prevents soil erosion . Since the vegetation cover is pulled down/destroyed, the long term effect of their activities is erosion.

questions.question_text: One similarity between Ghana and its neighbouring country, Togo is they both have

options.B: a coastline along the Atlantic ocean.

Which of the following entrepreneurial skills is most important for someone starting a business to acquire in order to help overcome unexpected challenges?
In 1957 the people of Ghana gained independence from the British colonial rule after a long political struggle. What was the main reason for Ghana's independence? To

options.A: make Ghanaian culture and traditions superior to the British
options.B: gain economic freedom and control our natural resources
options.C: adopt a new political system and form a government
options.D: unite with other African countries and form a single nation

questions.question_text: What type of climate, typically found near the equator is characterized by very high temperatures and very low rainfall?
People join social security and pension schemes in order to

options.A: raise money to satisfy political ambitions.
options.B: maintain standard of living in the cities.
options.D: supplement income for relations in the villages.

questions.question_text: Which of the following challenges did Ghana face soon after independence in 1957?

options.A: Dealing with internal conflicts and political instability
options.C: Building of new roads and drainage systems with limited funds

questions.question_text: It has been observed that the population of Ghana has been rising rapidly over the years. What is the primary reason for the rapidly growing population?

options.C: High birth rate and low death rate

questions.question_text: The following factors affect Ghana's ability to exploit its natural resources effectively except
What is the most common form of marriage in Ghana?
questions.explanation:
Monogamy , marriage to only one partner at a time, rather than multiple partners.

 Polygamy , marriage to more than one spouse at a time. The most typical forms of polygamy have been polygyny, in which cowives share a husband, or polyandry, in which co-husbands share a wife.

questions.question_text: The most significant attitude that helps to enhance self identity of the individual in the community is

options.A: engaging in peer teaching in school.
options.B: avoiding peer influence in the community.

questions.question_text: Which of the following fundamental human rights is protected by the 1992 constitution of Ghana? The right to
Which one of the following took place after the 27 th April, 1960 referendum?

options.A: The first political party was formed.
options.B: Kwame Nkrumah was inaugurated as Prime Minister.
options.D: Queen Elizabeth II was inaugurated as Prime Minister.

questions.explanation: A constitutional referendum was held in Ghana on 27 April 1960. The main issue was a change in the country's status from a constitutional monarchy with Elizabeth II as head of state, to a republic/democratic with a presidential system of government.

questions.question_text: Controlling plastic waste has become a major environmental concern to the city authorities in Ghana. Which of the following options best helps minimize the negative impact of plastic waste disposal problem?

options.A: Placing a ban on single use plastics

questions.question_text: Ghana is made up of people with different cultures. What is the primary benefit if all the people unite? It will
Which of the following scenarios, best describes an application of social security benefits?

options.A: A family member receives financial support after the death of the bread winner
options.B: A community receives funds for a new irrigation project
options.C: A teacher receives a scholarship to study abroad
options.D: An entrepreneur receives a loan to expand the business

questions.explanation: Social security is the protection that a society provides to individuals and households to ensure access to health care and to guarantee income security, particularly in cases of old age, unemployment, sickness, invalidity, work injury, maternity or loss of a breadwinner.

questions.question_text: You need to make a detailed map of a mountainous area. This map will be used by the people who enjoy outdoor activities and by people who manage the land. Which of the following options is the best way to make an accurate map?

options.B: Conduct field surveys and measure elevation

questions.question_text: Every Ghanaian citizen is expected to demonstrate commitment to democratic values. Which of the following actions best demonstrates the above expectation?

options.A: Criticizing the government on social media

questions.question_text: Respecting the rights of others and performing our responsibilities to the state help to promote
One way to maintain peace and security in your community is by
Two political events occurred in Ghana in 1960. One of the events was the presidential election. Which of the following political developments happened after the presidential election in 1960?

options.D: Ghana was divided into a northern and southern territory

questions.question_text: The best approach to help resolve the difference between political parties during general elections is

options.A: encouraging youth participation in national politics.
options.B: encouraging traditional leaders to play partisan politics.
options.C: allowing the formation of more political parties.
